markdown:
#  What happens when I import a Document Management file into the Drawings tool? 
##### Regional availability
The Document Management tool is available to accounts in the following countries: United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, and New Zealand. It is not yet available for Procore accounts in the U.S. To learn more, please reach out to your Procore point of contact.
##  Background 
The Drawings tool allows you to select Document Management files for viewing and marking up in the Drawings tool so you don’t have to bring that document through the full upload process all over again. Teams can upload and approve documents through established Document Management workflows and then use the ['attach files' selector](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/document-management-project/tutorials/import-document-management-files-into-the-drawings-tool) in the Drawings tool to find the document and quickly bring it into the Drawings tool with all the metadata attached. The rest of this FAQ discusses the data syncing limits and import indicators after the file is imported.
##  Answer 
### Syncing limitations
Any document markups or new document versions created in the Drawings tool are _not_ reflected in the file version that exists in Document Management tool. Likewise, any changes made to a file in the Document Management tool are _not_ reflected in the version that was previously imported into the Drawings tool. This is why it's recommended that teams only import _approved_ Document Management drawings into the Drawings tool.
For example, if the document is marked up or deleted in one tool, it will not markup or delete the copy that exists in the other tool.
Until Procore has a more integrated solution, this temporary import solution speeds upload times by taking advantage of the upload work already done in the Document Management tool. You can upload to the Document Management tool and then pull the file into the Drawings tool, where it will likely remain. To find out if a document was imported into the Drawings tool or if a document originated in the Document Management tool, look for the following indicators.
### Indicators in the Document Management tool
When a Document Management file is imported into the Drawings tool, that action is communicated back to the Document Management tool along with the date, time, and name of the user who did the import. This information shows in the following places:
  * **‘Submitted to Drawings Tool’ column.** The drawings ![Ad 4nxerzzidqtwepoqfu Gyj1j8gfeyl3bgrocllitiwul2ftgar3o0pkfrk5toih0gikuscw8uaadgvz4bwjpiiokzka4xerlfjq E7loksmhrokgazixwohgczqidtnrmowfdmopnvakeye6btrbxgvgeyxnlv1wdwcjje](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/039cb430c530a447db6101dde0236c50/4d01cead6a6ceedfb4d114b95463266a/AD_4nXeRZZiDQTWEpoQfU_GYj1J8gFEYl3BGRoCLliTIWuL2FTgar3o0PKfrK5tOIH0gIKuScw8UaAdgVZ4BWJpIIoKZKa4xeRLFJq-e7lokSmHrOKGaZixWOhgczQID?fm=webp&h=20) icon shows in the ‘Submitted to Drawings Tool’ column in the Document Management tool. Learn how to [choose columns](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/document-management-project/tutorials/configure-your-view-in-the-document-mangement-tool).
    * Hover over the icon to see the import date, time, and initiator.
    * You can also [filter by](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/document-management-project/tutorials/search-for-and-filter-documents-in-the-document-management-tool) by the ‘Submitted to Drawings Tool’ attribute to see all documents imported to the Drawings tool.
  * **Banners.** When viewing a document on web or mobile, a banner explains that the document was previously submitted to the Drawings tool, along with the import date, time, and initiator.
  * **Information panel.** When viewing a document, look for the 'Submitted to Drawings Tool' section.
  * **Change History.** When viewing a document, the Change History is accessed from the bottom of the Information panel.


### Indicators in the Drawings tool
When a Document Management file is imported into the Drawings tool, Procore also stores the date, time, and name of the importer as part of the metadata in the Drawings tool. This information shows in the file’s Change History, which is accessed via the 'Change History' tab when viewing the document.

  * [Why can't I turn off certain document 'Types' for the Document Management tool?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-why-cant-i-turn-off-certain-document-types-for-the-document-management-tool)
title: What happens when I import a Document Management file into the Drawings tool?
description: From the Drawings tool, users can open Document Management files, but only some data is shared after that.
